treatment_information.treatment_response: Patient is now four years old without apparent growth retardation, infection and autoimmune manifestations.
Given for recurrent wheezing and bronchiolitis.

treatment_timeline.event: Patient #1 admitted to the hospital with pneumonia and intravenous antibiotics were initiated.
Monthly intravenous immunoglobulin (IVIg) therapy and TMP-SMX for prophylaxis were started for patient #1.
Patient #1 required additional hospitalizations for pneumonia.
Inhaled budesonide and salbutamol were given to patient #1 for recurrent wheezing and bronchiolitis.
Monthly IVIg therapy and TMP-SMX for prophylaxis were started for patient #2.
Patient #2 treated for URTI once and suffered from recurrent diarrhea episodes.
Granulomatous skin lesions developed in patient #2 when she was six months old.

patient_outcome.final_status: Patient #1 is now four years old without apparent growth retardation, infection and autoimmune manifestations. Patient #2 is currently two years old and has apparent growth retardation.
patient_outcome.follow_up_details: Hematopoietic stem cell transplantation (HSCT) is planned for both patients from their 9/10 matched aunt.
